categories.risk_statement: Uploaded data, retrieved chunks, user messages, or tool metadata can try to override system instructions or trigger unauthorized tool use.

mitigations.title: Wrap untrusted content and scan prompt-injection patterns before LLM-bound use
mitigations.notes: User data and retrieved evidence are marked as untrusted content; tool metadata is scanned before planner exposure.

categories.residual_risk_note: Instruction separation reduces but cannot eliminate adversarial text risk, so write actions remain gated.

categories.risk_statement: Healthcare inputs may contain PHI, identifiers, secrets, or operational metadata that must not be exposed to unauthorized users, logs, or external providers.

mitigations.title: Classify and redact PHI before external provider handoff
mitigations.notes: External provider decisions are policy-backed and PHI allowance is explicit per surface.

mitigations.title: Block raw PHI in structured logs and secret health responses
mitigations.notes: Runtime diagnostics expose configured/missing status only, not secret values.

categories.residual_risk_note: Residual risk depends on administrator provider policy and user-provided content sensitivity.

categories.risk_statement: Models, tool catalogs, remote MCP exposure, retrieval sources, and dependency updates can introduce untrusted behavior or stale unsafe assumptions.

mitigations.title: Keep tool permissions and remote MCP exposure data-driven and reviewable
mitigations.notes: Remote MCP remains blocked unless deployment preconditions are satisfied.

mitigations.title: Represent healthcare retrieval sources with trust and license metadata
mitigations.notes: Source governance exists; full dependency SBOM and model provenance checks remain release-hardening work.

categories.residual_risk_note: The project has source/tool governance but still needs full release SBOM and model provenance automation.

categories.risk_statement: Unreviewed corpus material or poisoned retrieval chunks can bias validation, evidence, or assistant answers.

mitigations.title: Require source trust policies, source lifecycle states, and retrieval integrity checks
mitigations.notes: Source inventory distinguishes candidate, approved, deprecated, blocked, failed, and review-needed states.

categories.residual_risk_note: Source governance is present, but large-corpus ingestion should remain review-gated until evaluation coverage matures.

categories.risk_statement: LLM-generated plans, summaries, export descriptions, or UI text can contain unsafe instructions, unsupported claims, or malformed tool arguments.

mitigations.title: Validate generated plans and summaries before execution, storage, or display
mitigations.notes: Unsafe generated plans fall back to deterministic behavior instead of executing model output.

categories.residual_risk_note: Output validation is deterministic, but unsupported clinical claims still require evidence checks and human review.

categories.risk_statement: The assistant could be given too much authority to execute writes, approvals, exports, reindexing, or other operations without user consent.

mitigations.title: Allowlist tools and require explicit approval for write-gated actions
mitigations.notes: The model chooses among backend-declared tools; backend code owns actual execution and write gating.

categories.residual_risk_note: Write gates protect high-impact actions; future remote MCP must preserve per-user scope and consent.

categories.risk_statement: System prompts, private tool policy, secrets, runtime configuration, or hidden governance rules can leak through assistant responses or diagnostics.

mitigations.title: Keep secrets out of runtime diagnostics and use deterministic secret health checks
mitigations.notes: Secret health returns configured/required/remediation facts without secret values, token prefixes, or DSNs.

mitigations.title: Separate planner system instructions from untrusted user and tool content
mitigations.notes: Planner-visible payloads label untrusted data and expose only backend-approved tool schemas.

categories.residual_risk_note: Prompts are not treated as security boundaries; protected state must remain enforced by backend policies.

categories.risk_statement: Embedding mismatch, stale vector indexes, PHI-bearing embedding text, or weak retrieval filtering can produce unsafe evidence or leak sensitive context.

mitigations.title: Track embedding provider dimensions and require reindex alignment
mitigations.notes: Runtime settings expose embedding provider/model/dimensions and retrieval warns on incompatible vector dimensions.

categories.residual_risk_note: Embedding configuration is observable; production corpora still need scheduled retrieval evaluation and stale-index alerts.

categories.risk_statement: The assistant can produce unsupported, stale, or overconfident statements about healthcare data, standards, or validation results.

mitigations.title: Attach evidence support matrices and retrieval quality warnings to answers
mitigations.notes: Answers carry evidence summaries, support status, and warnings when evidence is weak or missing.

categories.residual_risk_note: Healthcare explanations still require human review and cannot be used as diagnosis or treatment guidance.

categories.risk_statement: Large uploads, repeated assistant requests, OCR jobs, embedding batches, and reindex operations can create cost spikes or denial-of-service pressure.

mitigations.title: Apply route rate limits and expensive-operation cost ceilings before provider calls
mitigations.notes: Limits are enforced before external LLM, vision OCR, embedding, and batch artifact creation paths.

categories.residual_risk_note: Configured limits reduce cost spikes; production still needs metrics dashboards and customer-specific quotas.
